exercises.title: Trace retry attempts for a transient failure
exercises.scenario: maxAttempts=3, client fails twice then succeeds.
exercises.expected_solution:
1. client.call() is invoked 3 times (all maxAttempts):
   Attempt 1: IOException (fail)
   Attempt 2: IOException (fail)
   Attempt 3: SUCCESS

2. Wait durations:
   Before attempt 2: 100ms (waitDuration)
   Before attempt 3: 200ms (100ms × 2.0 multiplier)
   Total added latency: ~300ms

3. Caller receives: the successful result from attempt 3.
   No exception thrown. Retry is transparent to the caller.

4. No — attempt 3 is the last attempt (maxAttempts=3).
   If attempt 3 also failed: fallback would be called (or exception propagated).

test_cases.expected:
1. client.call() is invoked 3 times (all maxAttempts):
   Attempt 1: IOException (fail)
   Attempt 2: IOException (fail)
   Attempt 3: SUCCESS

2. Wait durations:
   Before attempt 2: 100ms (waitDuration)
   Before attempt 3: 200ms (100ms × 2.0 multiplier)
   Total added latency: ~300ms

3. Caller receives: the successful result from attempt 3.
   No exception thrown. Retry is transparent to the caller.

4. No — attempt 3 is the last attempt (maxAttempts=3).
   If attempt 3 also failed: fallback would be called (or exception propagated).

hints: maxAttempts includes the first attempt
Exponential: delay × multiplier on each retry
Success on any attempt: stops retry and returns result

exercises.title: Complete the retry application.yml
exercises.scenario: Configure retry with exponential backoff and exception filtering.
exercises.starter_code:
resilience4j:
  retry:
    instances:
      visa-api:
        ___________: 3            # total attempts
        waitDuration: ____ms      # initial wait
        enable___________Backoff: true
        exponentialBackoffMultiplier: 2.0
        ___________Exceptions:    # only retry these
          - java.io.IOException
        ___________Exceptions:    # never retry these
          - com.razorpay.InvalidCardException
exercises.expected_solution:
resilience4j:
  retry:
    instances:
      visa-api:
        maxAttempts: 3
        waitDuration: 100ms
        enableExponentialBackoff: true
        exponentialBackoffMultiplier: 2.0
        retryExceptions:
          - java.io.IOException
        ignoreExceptions:
          - com.razorpay.InvalidCardException

blanks.hint: Total number of attempts including first

hints: retryExceptions: whitelist — only retry listed types
ignoreExceptions: blacklist — never retry listed types
enableExponentialBackoff: grows wait time each retry

exercises.title: Add @Retry to a service method
exercises.scenario: Retry transient HTTP failures with exponential backoff.
exercises.instructions:
Write SwiggyDeliveryService.estimateDelivery(orderId):
1. @Retry(name='delivery-api', fallbackMethod='defaultEstimate')
2. Calls delivery tracking API
3. Fallback: returns 45 minutes (default estimate)
4. Config in application.yml: maxAttempts=3, 200ms, exponential
5. Show why ConnectException should be retried but NotFoundException should not
hints: @Retry fallbackMethod: same as @CircuitBreaker — called after all retries exhausted
retryExceptions vs ignoreExceptions: transient vs permanent errors
Fallback receives the last exception from the final retry attempt

exercises.title: @RateLimiter to respect downstream API quota
exercises.scenario: Zerodha calls NSE with a 200 requests/second limit.
exercises.instructions:
Write ZerodhaNseService.submitOrder():
1. @RateLimiter(name='nse-api', fallbackMethod='orderThrottled')
2. Fallback: throw OrderThrottledException (pass exception up for retry)
3. application.yml: limitForPeriod=200, limitRefreshPeriod=1s, timeoutDuration=200ms
4. Show: how @RateLimiter protects NSE from being overwhelmed by Zerodha
hints: RateLimiter fallback: receives RequestNotPermittedException specifically
limitForPeriod: max calls in one limitRefreshPeriod
timeoutDuration: how long to wait for a permit before throwing

exercises.title: Test @Retry with a mock that fails then succeeds
exercises.scenario: Verify retry behavior: method called N times on N-1 failures + 1 success.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @SpringBootTest test:
1. Mock: fails with IOException twice, then returns 'SUCCESS'
2. Call the @Retry-protected method
3. Assert: the mock was called 3 times total
4. Assert: the returned value is 'SUCCESS'
5. Assert: no fallback was invoked
hints: thenThrow().thenThrow().thenReturn(): chain multiple behaviors
verify(mock, times(3)): assert exact call count
Fallback returns 45: verify expected fallback value, not mock value

exercises.title: Combine @Retry + @CircuitBreaker with correct order
exercises.scenario: Both annotations applied — order determines which failures the CB counts.
exercises.instructions:
Write Razorpay's VisaGatewayService:
1. @CircuitBreaker(name='visa', fallbackMethod='visaCBFallback') OUTER
2. @Retry(name='visa') INNER
3. Both share the same name for shared configuration
4. Fallback only on CB fallbackMethod
5. Comment explaining: why CB counts 1 failure per retry-sequence, not 3
hints: @CircuitBreaker outer, @Retry inner: top annotation is outer wrapper
CB records 1 failure per retry sequence
Wrong order: CB opens 3× faster than designed

exercises.title: Retry with randomized jitter backoff
exercises.scenario: Randomized jitter prevents all retrying threads from hitting the server at the same instant.
exercises.instructions:
Write FlipkartInventoryService.checkStock():
1. @Retry with exponentialRandomBackoff
2. application.yml: maxAttempts=4, initial 200ms, multiplier=1.5, randomFactor=0.5
3. Explain: without jitter, 1000 threads all retry at T=200ms → new spike
4. With jitter: retries spread across 100ms-300ms window
5. Test: mock that fails 3 times, succeeds on 4th
hints: Jitter: random factor prevents synchronized retry spikes
exponentialRandomBackoff: Resilience4j provides IntervalFunction.ofExponentialRandomBackoff()
maxAttempts=4: 1 original + 3 retries

exercises.title: Rate limiter with queue-based fallback
exercises.scenario: When rate limit exceeded, queue the request instead of dropping it.
exercises.instructions:
Write Swiggy's AnalyticsService:
1. @RateLimiter('swiggy-analytics'): 50 events/second
2. Fallback: add to bounded BlockingQueue (capacity 1000)
3. @Scheduled: drain queue and send events when capacity allows
4. Show: how this gives smooth analytics ingestion vs bursty direct calls
hints: BlockingQueue.offer(): non-blocking add, returns false if full
drainTo(list, maxElements): moves up to maxElements from queue to list
timeoutDuration=0ms: immediately fallback on rate limit (don't wait for permit)

exercises.title: Razorpay: @Retry + @CircuitBreaker integrated test
exercises.scenario: Test the combined retry + circuit breaker behavior end-to-end.
exercises.instructions:
Write 2 @SpringBootTest tests for VisaGatewayService:
1. Test A: fails 2 times, succeeds 3rd — verify called 3 times, returns success
2. Test B: fails all 3 attempts — verify called 3 times, fallback returns QUEUED status
3. Use @MockBean VisaClient, verify exact call counts
4. Reset circuit breaker in @BeforeEach
hints: registry.circuitBreaker(name).reset(): resets to CLOSED before each test
times(3): verify retry attempted all maxAttempts
CB fallback: 'QUEUED' status indicates payment was queued for later

exercises.title: Wrong annotation order — circuit opens too fast
exercises.scenario: Retry outside CircuitBreaker records 3 failures instead of 1.
exercises.expected_solution:
1. WRONG order: @Retry outer, @CB inner
   Each retry attempt passes through @CB independently.
   One call × 3 retry attempts = 3 CB failures recorded.

2. CB opens at ≥50% failure rate with min=5 calls.
   With wrong order: 2 original calls × 3 retries each = 6 CB failures.
   → After just 2 original calls: 6 failures → 60% failure rate → CIRCUIT OPENS.

3. CORRECT order: @CB outer, @Retry inner
   The entire retry sequence (3 attempts) = 1 CB failure.
   2 original calls × 1 CB failure each = 2 CB failures recorded.
   Need 5 failures to reach threshold → needs 5 original calls to open.

4. Ratio: wrong order opens at 2 calls; correct order opens at 5 calls.
   Wrong order: circuit opens 2.5× faster than designed!
   Impact: circuit opens during normal transient failures that retry would handle.

test_cases.expected:
1. WRONG order: @Retry outer, @CB inner
   Each retry attempt passes through @CB independently.
   One call × 3 retry attempts = 3 CB failures recorded.

2. CB opens at ≥50% failure rate with min=5 calls.
   With wrong order: 2 original calls × 3 retries each = 6 CB failures.
   → After just 2 original calls: 6 failures → 60% failure rate → CIRCUIT OPENS.

3. CORRECT order: @CB outer, @Retry inner
   The entire retry sequence (3 attempts) = 1 CB failure.
   2 original calls × 1 CB failure each = 2 CB failures recorded.
   Need 5 failures to reach threshold → needs 5 original calls to open.

4. Ratio: wrong order opens at 2 calls; correct order opens at 5 calls.
   Wrong order: circuit opens 2.5× faster than designed!
   Impact: circuit opens during normal transient failures that retry would handle.

hints: Wrong order: CB sees each retry attempt separately
Correct order: CB sees one result from entire retry sequence
Math: 3 retries × N calls = 3N CB failures instead of N

exercises.title: RateLimiter event listener for monitoring
exercises.scenario: Subscribe to rate limiter events for operational visibility.
exercises.instructions:
Write RateLimiterMonitor:
1. Subscribe to all rate limiter events (success + rejected)
2. Track: total calls, rejected calls, rejection rate
3. @Scheduled every minute: log metrics
4. Alert if rejection rate > 10% (too many rate limited calls)
5. Show: how this identifies when to increase limitForPeriod
hints: RateLimiterRegistry: similar to CircuitBreakerRegistry
onSuccess/onFailure: event types for rate limiter
getAndSet(0): atomic read + reset for rolling window metrics

exercises.title: Zerodha complete order submission resilience
exercises.instructions:
Write ZerodhaOrderSubmissionService:
1. @RateLimiter (200/s) outer
2. @CircuitBreaker (40% threshold) middle
3. @Retry (3 attempts) inner
4. Correct stacking order: RL → CB → Retry
5. Three distinct fallbacks for each annotation
6. Show: what each layer handles
hints: Stack order: RateLimiter (outer) > CircuitBreaker > Retry (inner)
Each layer handles a different failure mode
RateLimiter fallback receives RequestNotPermittedException specifically

exercises.title: Razorpay card retry with idempotency protection
exercises.scenario: Retry card charges safely using idempotency keys to prevent double charges.
exercises.instructions:
Write IdempotentChargeService:
1. @Retry(maxAttempts=3) on chargeWithRetry(idempotencyKey, request)
2. Each retry uses the SAME idempotency key (prevents double charge)
3. If Visa returns 'already_processed' for the idempotency key: return cached result
4. ignoreExceptions: CardDeclinedException (permanent — don't retry)
5. Test: mock returns 'already_processed' on 2nd attempt → idempotency handled
hints: Same idempotency key on all retries: Visa recognizes it as same request
Cache result on success: idempotency cache prevents re-processing
ignoreExceptions: CardDeclinedException — don't retry declined cards

exercises.title: BookMyShow payment retry with backoff and test
exercises.scenario: Full retry implementation for payment processing with tests.
exercises.instructions:
Build BookMyShow PaymentRetryService:
1. @CircuitBreaker + @Retry on processPayment()
2. 3 attempts, 100ms exponential backoff
3. Retry: IOException, SocketTimeoutException
4. Ignore: CardDeclinedException, InsufficientFundsException
5. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): transient success, permanent fail skips retries, fallback correct
hints: ignoreExceptions configured in application.yml: CardDeclinedException not retried
times(1) for CardDeclined: verify only 1 attempt made
fallback status contains 'PAYMENT_FAILED': check with contains(), not equals()

exercises.title: Complete Zerodha resilience: RateLimiter + Retry + CircuitBreaker synthesis
exercises.scenario: Full M19 Topic 2 synthesis — all three resilience patterns stacked.
exercises.instructions:
Build complete ZerodhaResilienceDemo:
1. Three stacked annotations on submitOrder: @RateLimiter (200/s) → @CircuitBreaker (40%) → @Retry (3 attempts)
2. Separate application.yml configs for each resilience4j type
3. Event listeners: RL rejection count, CB state transitions, Retry attempts count
4. Three @SpringBootTest tests: rate limit triggers, retry succeeds on 3rd attempt, CB opens after failures

All 3 tests pass.
hints: Stack order: @RateLimiter (outer) > @CircuitBreaker > @Retry (inner)
@PostConstruct listeners: run after all Resilience4j beans initialized
cbOpened counter: easier to assert than direct state (state may vary by test timing)
